methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.9x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Herasight is a seed-stage biotech company founded by Michael Christensen that has raised $5M+. The valuation analysis draws on a comparative universe of 3 public biotech peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers in Biotech & Health, including companies such as Colossal Biosciences, Abridge, and Chai Discovery.

Stage-progression methodology is the applicable triangulation approach here. Using the $5M total raised as a proxy for current valuation and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier for progression from Seed to the next stage yields an implied valuation of $12.5M. Peer-median analysis is not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparable datapoints within the narrow peer set—while 8 private peers exist in the sector, none met the minimum threshold of 3 comparable companies filtered by stage and valuation tier. Public-comp anchoring via the sector's 6.9x median price-to-sales multiple cannot be applied without Herasight's revenue figures, which are not on file.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $11M to $14M, with a median of $12M. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single methodological pillar. Key uncertainties include the absence of revenue data, which limits applicability of public market multiples, and the scarcity of tightly-matched private comparables at seed stage. The valuation should be revisited once more detailed operating metrics or revenue guidance become available.
